prompt: Please find the item “Recycled packing tape” at Warehouse C and tell me its record ID and current restock date. This is only a check for now: do not change, cancel, or notify anything. If the lookup yields more than one exact match, stop and ask me which record I mean before making any change.

follow_up_turns.content: That is the right item. For that same record only, set the restock date to 2026-11-24; keep notifications off.
Small correction: set the restock date to 2026-12-10, not 2026-11-24. This replaces only the value from my last message; keep the same record and do not notify anyone.

stages.purpose: Resolve one exact target by name and location while leaving nearby distractors untouched.
Apply the first requested restock date only to the resolved target.
Replace the superseded restock date on that same target with the user's correction.

response_constraints: Keep every follow-up tied to the record resolved in the preceding stage; do not switch to a similarly named record.
Do not report a lookup or update as successful unless the corresponding tool result establishes it.
After the correction, report the final restock date and make clear that the first requested restock date was superseded.
If initial resolution returns multiple exact candidates, ask the user to choose and do not execute an update until one is selected.
